PORTIONS OF ROOF BLOWN OFF APARTMENT BUILDINGS ON DUBLIN TRAIL. TWO CARS WERE ALSO DAMAGED WHEN GARAGE DOORS BLEW IN.

A TORNADO, RATED EF1, TOUCHED DOWN JUST BEFORE 1045 PM ABOUT 4 MILES WEST-NORTHWEST OF MENASHA. THE TORNADO PRODUCED SPORATIC DAMAGE ALONG A NARROW PATH ALMOST 1/2 MILE LONG. THE HEAVIEST DAMAGE OCCURRED IN AN APARTMENT COMPLEX. FOUR BUILDINGS WERE AFFECTED IN THE COMPLEX, TWO OF WHICH LOST PORTIONS OF THEIR ROOFS. THE TORNADO WAS WITNESSED BY FOUR PEOPLE AS IT MOVED EAST-NORTHEAST ALONG THE ROAD THAT CUTS THROUGH THE COMPLEX.
